reasoning: Starlink was officially granted a 10-year license in Lesotho on April 14, 2025. The resolution date of July 1, 2025, allows ~78 days (2.5 months) for the service to launch and the map to update to 'Available'. Recent Starlink launches in Africa range from taking just days (like the DRC in early May 2025) or <1 month (Eswatini), up to 3-4 months (Botswana and Zimbabwe). The rapid 2-month processing time by the Lesotho Communications Authority shows strong regulatory enthusiasm. While there is minor pushback from a local rights group ('Section 2') demanding 30% local equity, the official license has already been issued. Given Starlink's accelerated deployment speed recently, 2.5 months is highly likely to be enough time to go live, bringing the probability to around 70%.
